marketState: neutral, tilting bullish on weather/positioning but capped by storage surplus
summary: Front-month NG at $2.88 is trading essentially at fair value (spread -$0.04) while the fair-value curve itself is climbing, to $2.94 in 7 days and $3.05 by +21D, as cooling demand builds sharply: the 8-16 day forecast is +31% vs the 5-year average and already sits above the historical max range. Managed money is near a 5-year extreme net-short (8th percentile), which raises squeeze risk if that heat demand materializes. Pulling the other way, storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average with this week's build surprising 22 Bcf bearish, and seasonality reliability is low (59pp spread) with only a 38% historical odds of higher prices 3-4 weeks out. Short-term technicals are bearish across 1h-1w (50<200 EMA, mostly negative MACD) even though the 5m/15m are oversold (RSI 16.7, below lower Bollinger), so there is no clean directional edge over the next 1-10 days. Futures curve data is missing from this snapshot, removing one short-term corroborating input.

longTerm.rationale: 8-16D cooling demand is +31% vs 5y and above the historical max, and the fair-value curve is rising toward $3.05 by +21D while price sits only at/near fair now, leaving room to catch up. Managed money is at the 8th percentile net-short over 5 years, a stretched positioning that adds squeeze risk on any confirmation of the extreme heat.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surprises keep printing bearish and 8-16D demand outlook rolls back toward/under the 5y average, or price closes below $2.55 (below the rising fair-value band's lower edge)

shortTerm.rationale: Price is basically at fair value now (spread -$0.04, status FAIR) so there is no near-term mispricing edge, and technicals conflict: 1h-1w trend is bearish (50<200 EMA) while 5m/15m are oversold (RSI 16.7, price below lower Bollinger). Futures curve data is unavailable to corroborate either side.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a 1h/4h RSI reclaim above 40 with MACD turning positive on the 1d for a bounce long, or (b) a confirmed daily close below $2.75 with bearish MACD holding for a momentum short toward the lower fair-value band.
